relevant_files.summary: buildInitFabricPlan writes .fabric/bootstrap/README.md, .fabric/INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md, .fabric/rules, events, forensic, skills and hooks. buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown derives taxonomy from forensic topology and candidate files.
deriveAgentsMetaLayer returns L0 for AGENTS.md and .fabric/bootstrap/README.md, L1 for _cross, otherwise derives by directory depth.
computeRulesBasedAgentsMeta creates a special bootstrap node and scans .fabric/rules markdown files for stable ids, descriptions, sections, layer and topology.
Contains L0 bootstrap, L0 root, and L1 _cross/cocos-creator nodes.
Contains all four structured rule sections.

patterns.description: .fabric/bootstrap/README.md is treated as L0 but is not a normal .fabric/rules source file.
Initial taxonomy is generated from forensic topology key dirs and candidate files.
Structured section parser is designed for .fabric/rules files and supports Chinese content under stable English section names.

key_findings: Layer information exists in agents.meta.json but is not obvious from the file tree.
Direct rules/l0|l1|l2 migration would require changes to layer derivation and compatibility behavior.
Bootstrap README currently violates structured rule section shape by design because it is a bootstrap contract.
INITIAL_TAXONOMY is auto-generated before fabric-init follow-up; better treated as draft evidence, not final taxonomy.
